key_points: Marginalisation means pushing certain individuals or groups to the edge of society
Marginalised groups have fewer opportunities, resources and power in society
Some communities such as Adivasis and Muslims have faced marginalisation in India
Marginalisation can occur due to social, economic or cultural differences
Marginalised groups often face discrimination and inequality
The Constitution provides rights and protections to support marginalised communities
examples: Many Adivasi communities depend on forests for their livelihood
Some minority groups face discrimination in education and employment
Government policies aim to improve opportunities for marginalised communities
Social awareness can help reduce discrimination and inequality
formulas: Marginalisation → Social inequality + Discrimination + Lack of opportunities
Protection of groups → Constitution + Laws + Government policies
exam_tips: Mention examples of marginalised communities such as Adivasis and Muslims
Explain how discrimination leads to marginalisation
Write how the Constitution protects the rights of marginalised groups
